content.raw:
I am a licensed psychotherapist (California & Pennsylvania), certified health coach, personal trainer, and certified psychedelic-assisted therapist.  Depending on my client's needs, I offer a blend of modalities that may integrate embodiment, mindfulness, movement, breathwork, and nutritional and lifestyle guidance.  My mission is to offer personalized, high-quality holistic therapy that nourishes both the mind and the body so that my clients can feel lighter, healthier, and have more moments of presence, connection, joy, and awe in their lives. 

Over the past 20 years as a therapist, I have found that for many, traditional therapy can fall short; as such, I am thrilled to be trained and educated in using psychedelics as a complementary modality that can accelerate change and offer opportunities for deep healing.  

I work with high-achieving clients who have a harsh inner critical voice that keeps them from feeling never-good-enough.  Their inner critic perpetuates a heaviness that keeps them from feeling accomplished, despite evidence to the contrary.  

I also work with clients experiencing loss or anticipating loss.  These losses include, but are not limited to, the loss of a loved one, a relationship, a job, a home, and one's health.  I  have found that psychedelic-assisted therapy can not only help with deep releases that aid in processing despair, but it can also help one more gently and holistically navigate a new reality with more compassion, grace, and connection.

I am a licensed psychotherapist (California & Pennsylvania), certified health coach, personal trainer, and certified psychedelic-assisted therapist. Depending on my client’s needs, I offer a blend of modalities that may integrate embodiment, mindfulness, movement, breathwork, and nutritional and lifestyle guidance. My mission is to offer personalized, high-quality holistic therapy that nourishes both the mind and the body so that my clients can feel lighter, healthier, and have more moments of presence, connection, joy, and awe in their lives.

Over the past 20 years as a therapist, I have found that for many, traditional therapy can fall short; as such, I am thrilled to be trained and educated in using psychedelics as a complementary modality that can accelerate change and offer opportunities for deep healing.

I work with high-achieving clients who have a harsh inner critical voice that keeps them from feeling never-good-enough. Their inner critic perpetuates a heaviness that keeps them from feeling accomplished, despite evidence to the contrary.

I also work with clients experiencing loss or anticipating loss. These losses include, but are not limited to, the loss of a loved one, a relationship, a job, a home, and one’s health. I have found that psychedelic-assisted therapy can not only help with deep releases that aid in processing despair, but it can also help one more gently and holistically navigate a new reality with more compassion, grace, and connection.

content.raw: Board-certified in Adult Psychiatry, I earned my MD from Wake Forest School of Medicine and completed residency and Chief Resident year at St. Vincent’s Hospital in Manhattan through New York Medical College. With over 20 years in clinical practice, I have worked in integrated primary care, academic, VA, and private clinic settings. While providing psychiatric care to patients at Lifestance Health and Palo Alto Medical Foundation, I also consulted with Primary Care doctors and supervised Nurse Practitioners. While working at the VA New York Harbor Healthcare System treating veterans with Chronic PTSD and other mental illnesses, I also supervised residents and medical students. I continue to supervise Nurse Practitioners and collaborate regularly with Primary Care doctors.  I am a Certified Integrative Psychiatry Provider.  I am also the author of peer-reviewed research on treating depression and obesity in the Primary Care setting using an integrative model of psychiatric care.

content.raw:
Something meaningful in your life isn't working — even though on the outside, things look fine. You've tried therapy, you have insight into your patterns, but something still isn't shifting. That's exactly who I work with.

I'm a licensed therapist in Pasadena, California. Before becoming a therapist, I spent most of my adult life as a successful executive in investment management — driven, accomplished, and always moving. What I didn't realize until my 50s was that the constant motion was keeping me from slowing down enough to feel. Therapy changed that profoundly enough that I left my career, went back to school, and became a therapist myself.

My approach draws on depth-oriented (psychodynamic) therapy, IFS (Internal Family Systems), and Somatic EMDR. I work at the level where patterns actually live — not just in your thinking, but in your body and emotional experience.

I am certified as a psychedelic-assisted therapy provider through the Integrative Psychiatry Institute (IPI, 2023). I offer ketamine-assisted psychotherapy (KAP) in collaboration with Journey Clinical, as well as psychedelic integration therapy for clients processing experiences from ketamine, plant medicines, or other expanded states — whether recent or from years ago.

content.raw:
I enjoy providing compassionate, comprehensive care through both in-person and telehealth visits. I work with individuals experiencing depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, PTSD, substance use disorders, and other mental health challenges.
I take a whole-person approach to treatment, recognizing that mental health and physical health are deeply connected. In addition to thoughtful medication management, I incorporate lifestyle, nutrition, and mind-body strategies when appropriate to support lasting wellness.
I believe in creating a safe, collaborative space where patients feel heard, respected, and empowered. I also value strong partnerships with fellow healthcare professionals to ensure coordinated, high-quality care. My goal is simple: to help people feel better, function well, and move toward meaningful, sustainable recovery.
I welcome the opportunity to connect with patients, colleagues, and community partners who share a commitment to integrative, patient-centered mental health care.

content.raw: I am a queer & neurodivergent somatic trauma therapist located in Vancouver, BC. I am passionate about the use of psychedelics for trauma healing. I have a background working in behavioural therapy for 10 years, as well as practicing massage therapy and teaching yoga. I am passionate about hollistic and integrative mental health. I use psychedelics in the treatment of PTSD, trauma related disorders, treatement resistant depression, and for mental health exploration. I have a focus on working with neurodivergent and 2SLGBTQIA+ communities. I have a Master of Counselling Psychology and practice as a Registered Clinical Counsellor and Canadian Certified Counsellor. I am a Certified Psychedelic Assisted Therapy provider with IPI as well as a level 2 trained Somatic Experiencing practitioner. Please contact me directly for more information about Psychedelic Assisted Therapy.

My goal is to partner with each patient to create a personalized path toward healing, resilience, and improved quality of life.

content.raw:
Welcome to Michigan Progressive Health, a physician-led clinic dedicated to providing integrative ketamine treatment that improves the lives of our patients.  We are proud providers of integrative psychedelic psychiatry.  This holistic approach to mental health combines conventional and complementary therapies, ensuring a comprehensive treatment tailored to individual needs.  We offer Spravato, IV ketamine, medication management and psychotherapy.

MPH is also Michigan’s leader in ketamine assisted psychotherapy. With the guidance of one of our therapists, our patients can use ketamine to gain new insights and perspectives. Our patients routinely experience breakthroughs with this treatment, leading them closer to mental wellness.

We have locations in Ann Arbor and Royal Oak, Michigan.  We accept BCBS traditional and PPO plans and Aetna.

content.raw:
Aliza Madden, MSN, PMHNP-BC is a Board-Certified Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner trained at Columbia University who provides Psychodynamic Psychotherapy, medication management, and integrative psychiatric support for individuals ages 12 and up. Aliza helps her patients explore and work through their emotional challenges so that they can improve their relationships and overall mental health, change negative patterns of behavior, and find the freedom to live more fulfilling and meaningful lives. Sessions are offered in person in Ridgefield, CT or via telehealth for residents of New York and Connecticut. 

Aliza earned her Master of Science in Psychiatric Nursing at Columbia University in 2018 and worked in residential, inpatient, and community-based outpatient mental health settings prior to opening her private practice. Aliza is highly trained to diagnose and treat mental health conditions including depression, anxiety, trauma and PTSD, depression and mood disorders, obsessive-compulsive disorders, and attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder using a combination of psychotherapy, medication, and integrative support depending on each individual’s needs and preferences. 

Aliza is a graduate of the Child, Adolescent, and Parent Psychotherapy Training Program at the Westchester Center for the Study of Psychoanalysis and Psychotherapy (WCSPP). She is also a Certified Integrative Psychiatric Provider through the Institute for Integrative Psychiatry.

Sessions are offered in person in Ridgefield, CT or via telehealth for residents of New York and Connecticut.

Aliza earned her Master of Science in Psychiatric Nursing at Columbia University in 2018 and worked in residential, inpatient, and community-based outpatient mental health settings prior to opening her private practice. Aliza is highly trained to diagnose and treat mental health conditions including depression, anxiety, trauma and PTSD, depression and mood disorders, obsessive-compulsive disorders, and attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder using a combination of psychotherapy, medication, and integrative support depending on each individual’s needs and preferences.

Aliza is a graduate of the Child, Adolescent, and Parent Psychotherapy Training Program at the Westchester Center for the Study of Psychoanalysis and Psychotherapy (WCSPP). She is also a Certified Integrative Psychiatric Provider through the Institute for Integrative Psychiatry.

content.raw:
I specialize in providing conventional and integrative psychiatric care to individuals navigating the high-stakes demands of modern life, including but not limited to college / graduate students, entrepreneurs, innovators, investors, corporate leaders, consultants, scientists, engineers, physicians, allied health professionals, and lawyers.  

My unique background combines rigorous medical training with a decade of high-level strategic consulting, giving me an unparalleled perspective on the challenges that professionals face across different fields and stages of their career.  Having come from a similar background, I am also very familiar with the various personal life stressors and the setbacks, disappointments and even traumatic losses that many ambitious, conscientious, and oftentimes idealistic or perfectionistic individuals can find especially difficult to face along their own journeys - some are externally imposed and some are internally imposed, but thankfully, all are ultimately surmountable with the right support and strategies.

Amidst these inevitable challenges of life, my goal is to help lift you up from simply surviving to thriving by aligning your mental wellness and internal resources with your self-defined ambitions to reach a fulfilling sense of success.  Because the truth is, just like with everything else, we all need to invest the time and effort to learn to live fully.

content.raw:
Heidi J. Dalzell, PsyD, is a Licensed Clinical Psychologist, specializing in and trauma, EMDR, eating disorders and psychedelic assisted psychotherapy. She has 30+ years of direct clinical experience. Dr. Dalzell is a graduate of the Integrative Psychiatry Institute, with certifications in Ketamine Assisted Therapy, Psilocybin Assisted therapy and as a MAPs-trained MDMA provider. She provides peer groups and mentoring through Journey Clinical.

In addition to completing a certificate in Psychedelic Assisted Therapy, Dr. Dalzell is an author and frequent presenter at professional conferences. She has been at the forefront of using psychedelic medicines to support embodiment, body image and recovery from eating disorders. 

Dr. Dalzell has a busy private practice, offering Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy and preparation and integration of plant medicines. An important part of this work has been her connection to and work with plant medicines. She enjoys sharing her knowledge and experiences in this realm with others.

content.raw:
Hi, I’m Lauren! I’m passionate about supporting adults on their journey toward healing, growth, and emotional well-being. With five years of experience in counseling, I have had the privilege of working with a diverse range of clients, helping them navigate challenges such as trauma, mental health concerns, and substance use.

I approach therapy with a heart for holistic wellness and a commitment to client-centered care. My work is grounded in compassion, curiosity, and collaboration, providing a safe space for you to explore your experiences, build resilience, and develop practical tools for navigating life’s challenges.

In addition to traditional therapy support, I am trained and certified in psychedelic integration by the Integrative Psychiatry Institute, helping clients process and integrate transformative experiences in meaningful, lasting ways. Whether you’re seeking support for anxiety, depression, trauma, life transitions, or personal growth, my goal is to walk alongside you with understanding and guidance as you create positive, lasting change.

yoast_head_json.og_description: Hi, I’m Lauren! I’m passionate about supporting adults on their journey toward healing, growth, and emotional well-being. With five years of experience in counseling, I have had the privilege of working with a diverse range of clients, helping them navigate...

content.raw:
I come to this work with both lived experience and professional training, but more importantly, with my heart. I believe healing is not about fixing what is broken — it’s about remembering the parts of ourselves we had to hide, protect, or silence along the way. My role is to help you reconnect with those parts with compassion, curiosity, and love.

I am a Licensed Professional Counselor in Texas and certified in Psychedelic-Assisted Therapy through the Integrative Psychiatry Institute. I have facilitated hundreds of Ketamine-Assisted Psychotherapy sessions and have supported many psilocybin ceremonies in retreat and therapeutic settings. In addition to my therapy practice in Texas, I also support clients nationwide as a psychedelic preparation and integration coach, helping people work safely and intentionally with psychedelic medicines wherever they are.

My approach combines deep presence with evidence-based trauma healing. I integrate Internal Family Systems (IFS), EMDR, somatic work, and LENS Neurofeedback to support the nervous system, the emotional body, and the spiritual self — because real healing happens on all levels.

In our work together, I take time to truly understand your story, your wounds, your strengths, and the parts of you that learned to survive. We move slowly, gently, and intentionally through preparation, guided psychedelic experiences (ketamine in Texas), and ongoing integration. I hold space with steadiness and care — not pushing, not forcing, but allowing what is ready to unfold, unfold.

I specialize in working with people who have tried so hard for so long — those who feel stuck, overwhelmed, numb, lost, or disconnected from who they are. People who sense that talk therapy alone hasn’t reached the deeper layers. People who are ready to heal at the root.

If you are longing for clarity, release, reconnection, or a return to your true self, I am here to walk with you. Not as the expert who holds your answers — but as someone who believes in your inner healing intelligence and knows how to help you access it.

You are not broken.
You are becoming.

qualifications:
Allison Wells MD MBA FASA FACHE CPE CMQ

Dr Allison Wells is a licensed, board-certified anesthesiologist with expertise in anesthesia from ambulatory care to complex surgical cases, and extensive experience in ketamine infusions and outpatient procedures. Her passion is helping patients achieve the best-possible outcomes in a safe, professional and comfortable manner.
 
Dr Wells is an experience-leader in interventional treatments for mood disorders and pain conditions. She co-founded one of the first focused clinics providing ketamine infusions in the country, and the first in Houston, providing over 12,000 infusions since 2015. She actively contributes to the field with research, advocacy, and efforts that expand access to care.
 
Dr Wells holds degrees from Swarthmore, Baylor College of Medicine, and UMass Amherst, and she trained at Baylor College of Medicine and Harvard Medical School. She is a Clinical Assistant Professor in the Department of Psychiatry and Behavioral Sciences at Baylor College of Medicine, a district director of the Texas Society of Anesthesiologists, a founding board member of The Ketamine TaskForce, and an executive board member of Legacy Health. She is a fellow of the American Society Anesthesiologists, a fellow of the American College of Healthcare Executives, a Certified Physician Executive, and a member of the American Society of Anesthesiologists, the Texas Society of Anesthesiologists and the Texas Medical Association.

content.raw:
Hello, I’m Amanda, and I truly understand how overwhelming life’s challenges can feel. With over 17 years of experience as a psychotherapist, I’m here to support you through your most difficult times and guide you toward healing, growth, and lasting happiness.

Are you struggling with:

Anxiety or panic attacks?
Persistent depression?
The aftermath of sexual trauma or PTSD?
Relationship issues?
Low self-esteem?
 

You don’t have to face these struggles alone. At Thrive, we’re dedicated to creating a safe, nurturing environment where you can begin to heal and rediscover hope.

I specialize in trauma-informed care and offer support in:

Recovering from sexual trauma
Treating PTSD
Managing anxiety and depression
Navigating relationship challenges
Exploring innovative therapies like Ketamine-Assisted Psychotherapy
Awaiting FDA approval for MDMA-Assisted and Psilocybin-Assisted Therapies (where legally available)
 

As a certified psychedelic-assisted psychotherapist through the Integrative Psychiatry Institute, I provide cutting-edge treatments designed to help you move past your pain and embrace a more fulfilling future.

Imagine feeling safe in your own skin, building healthy relationships, and looking forward to what lies ahead. It’s possible. Together, we’ll work on:

Healing from trauma and PTSD
Overcoming anxiety and depression
Enhancing intimacy and relationships
Reclaiming your inner strength and resilience
 

You deserve to feel whole and joyful again. Let’s take the first step together towards reclaiming your life and finding true happiness.

Schedule your confidential session today. Your path to healing and empowerment starts here, and I’m here to help you unlock your inner strength and thrive.

content.raw:
I support seekers and space holders alike in healing. My work is rooted in trauma-focused, liberation-centered, and relational care, blending the wisdom of expressive and collective practices, somatics, parts work, and other ancient and emerging healing practices with the compassionate witnessing and presence I bring to support others in their expanded-state experiences.

Whether I am facilitating psycholytic ketamine-assisted therapy sessions while utilizing a blend of EMDR/IFS, supporting deep inner exploration in preparation, medicine macrodosing, and integration work, or providing consultation to other clinicians who desire community and guidance on how to be in this space ethically, authentically, and with intentionality, I aim to hold space that is warm, loving, and attuned to the wholeness of each person I have the honor of walking alongside in this sacred, special work.

content.raw:
Amber Kerby is a licensed marriage and family therapist in Iowa and New Hampshire.  She maintains a private practice that offers a hybrid of telehealth and in office services.  Her experience in the field of mental health ranges from home-based therapy and school-based counseling to clinical work in private practice.

As the founder of Non Ordinary Therapy, LLC, Amber is expanding access to novel approaches in mental health therapy.  She is trained in Internal Family Systems (IFS) and utilizes this approach to assist clients in changing perspectives and deepening understanding of themselves and their symptoms.

Additionally, Amber is the first LMFT in Iowa to offer Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy in her private practice.  Ketamine produces a shift in consciousness such as an expanded state of awareness and access to novel ways of viewing life concerns.  Providing therapeutic support and interventions before, during, and after ketamine treatment increases the potential for lasting change.

Her certification as psychedelic assisted therapy provider extends beyond ketamine to provide psychedelic integration in a therapeutic context.  In addition, Amber provides education, consultation, and training for clinicians and mental health organizations around ketamine assisted psychotherapy and psychedelic integration as a therapeutic intervention.
content.rendered:
Amber Kerby is a licensed marriage and family therapist in Iowa and New Hampshire. She maintains a private practice that offers a hybrid of telehealth and in office services. Her experience in the field of mental health ranges from home-based therapy and school-based counseling to clinical work in private practice.

As the founder of Non Ordinary Therapy, LLC, Amber is expanding access to novel approaches in mental health therapy. She is trained in Internal Family Systems (IFS) and utilizes this approach to assist clients in changing perspectives and deepening understanding of themselves and their symptoms.

Additionally, Amber is the first LMFT in Iowa to offer Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy in her private practice. Ketamine produces a shift in consciousness such as an expanded state of awareness and access to novel ways of viewing life concerns. Providing therapeutic support and interventions before, during, and after ketamine treatment increases the potential for lasting change.

Her certification as psychedelic assisted therapy provider extends beyond ketamine to provide psychedelic integration in a therapeutic context. In addition, Amber provides education, consultation, and training for clinicians and mental health organizations around ketamine assisted psychotherapy and psychedelic integration as a therapeutic intervention.

content.raw: Adam O'Neil, PhD, is a licensed clinical psychologist in Colorado. He has dual training in both clinical psychology as well as sport and performance psychology, and he has worked with athletes and performers on mental health and psychological performance for over 15 years. Adam completed the Psychedelic Assisted Therapy program at IPI (6th cohort) in January, 2025, and is currently in the process of applying to become a Licensed Clinical Facilitator. Additionally, Adam is planning to apply for ownership of a State of Colorado Licensed Healing Center, expected in mid-2025. Stay tuned!

Additionally, Adam is planning to apply for ownership of a State of Colorado Licensed Healing Center, expected in mid-2025. Stay tuned!

content.raw:
Michelle Briggs is a patient-focused nurse practitioner specializing in integrative psychiatry. 
Michelle began her career at Johns Hopkins Hospital in Baltimore and has more than thirty years of experience helping patients achieve their mental, spiritual, and physical healthcare goals. 
​She is compassionate, insightful, and determined to help people access their inner healing journey.
content.rendered:

content.raw: My early training is in Internal Medicine and Psychiatry with a fellowship in Geriatric Psychiatry. After my fellowship I set up my outpatient practice and have been working for over 16 years now. I have also trained in Integrative Psychiatry through IPI and am currently completing my fellowship in Functional Medicine through the Institute of Functional Medicine. I have started a Trauma treatment certification as well as women’s health with the IPI. I have just completed my training with IPI in Psychedelic assisted psychotherapy.

content.raw: Kate Schroeder, LPC, LMHC, NCC, is a Licensed Psychotherapist and founder of Transformation Counseling, LLC. With over 28 years of experience, she specializes in depression, anxiety, C-PTSD, and working with adult survivors of childhood trauma and abuse. Kate is certified in the Myers-Briggs Type Indicator and has extensive training in the Enneagram system of personality typing, and is trained in Somatic Experiencing and Internal Family Systems. She is also a certified Body-Centered Gestalt practitioner and a Certified Psychedelic Assisted Therapist from the Integrative Psychiatry Institute. Her clinical background includes experience as a school counselor, mental health therapist in an urban university’s counseling center, individual, couples, group and family therapist, and clinical research. Within these settings, Kate has provided individual, group and family counseling for clients experiencing difficulties in areas including crisis intervention, depression and anxiety, adjustment and transitional concerns, acculturation and multicultural issues, family and childhood conflicts, interpersonal relationships, grief and trauma, eating disorders, LGBTQ issues, self-efficacy, career exploration and various other interpersonal conflicts. Kate also holds certification as a massage therapist, physical trainer and physical education teacher.

She is also a certified Body-Centered Gestalt practitioner and a Certified Psychedelic Assisted Therapist from the Integrative Psychiatry Institute. Her clinical background includes experience as a school counselor, mental health therapist in an urban university’s counseling center, individual, couples, group and family therapist, and clinical research. Within these settings, Kate has provided individual, group and family counseling for clients experiencing difficulties in areas including crisis intervention, depression and anxiety, adjustment and transitional concerns, acculturation and multicultural issues, family and childhood conflicts, interpersonal relationships, grief and trauma, eating disorders, LGBTQ issues, self-efficacy, career exploration and various other interpersonal conflicts. Kate also holds certification as a massage therapist, physical trainer and physical education teacher.

content.raw:
Most people, at one time or another need the support of a therapist. It can be one of the best gifts you can give yourself. Perhaps you are struggling with past trauma, anxiety, depression, addiction, grief or having difficulty in your relationships. We are often responding to life and the people around us with outdated coping strategies. Through my integrative approach I will help you connect to your inner healing intelligence, develop self awareness and emotional regulation skills so that instead of being hijacked by your emotions you are empowered to respond to life with more clarity and choice. 

It is my passion to support people on their path to well being and I have been in private practice for over 20 years. My holistic approach includes KAP, Brainspotting, IFS, Hypnotherapy, CBT, and the wisdom of mindfulness techniques. I can help you get to what lies beneath the surface so that any sabotaging beliefs, maladaptive patterns, past wounds and traumas stop running your life.

Therapy is a collaborative process. I understand how vulnerable it can be to open yourself up and look within. I create a nonjudgemental and compassionate space so that my clients feel safe and supported to the deep work they need to create the beautiful life they deserve. 

I also offer KAP Retreats in a space designed to give my clients the ability to take time for themselves and for us to do deeper work. Clients arrive on Friday where we do a prep session, then the medicine session on Saturday and Integration on Sunday.  Traditional two hour KAP sessions are also available as well as the above format during the week. This is a powerful way to fully benefit from the gifts of Ketamine Assisted Therapy.

content.raw: Hi! I am a licensed clinical psychologist offering different forms of KAP around the Boston area. I work from a trauma lens using narrative therapy and parts-work, and I may include EMDR and/ or vagus nerve toning (Safe and Sound Protocol) in my treatment. I am always looking to network and join forces with others in the field, so please don't hesitate to reach out!

content.raw:
My journey into working as a Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner (PMHNP) with a holistic perspective began while living and working as a wildlife biologist in the Wyoming mountains. I was faced with my own mental health challenges especially after losing my mother unexpectedly to cancer. My world unraveled but also opened up after her passing as I explored my past and present life and I knew almost immediately that I wanted to help others in their journey of achieving mental wellness. 

Since becoming a PMHNP, I honed my skills as a holistic healer by completing numerous trainings and fellowships in integrative and precision medicine so that I could offer a variety of tools to my clients in their own journey of achieving mental wellness. My focus is on nutrition, gut health, and supplement use for mental health and wellness. 

Today I strive to offer hope to my clients that there are solutions to our mental health struggles. My goal is to work collaboratively with my clients in identifying these solutions and to assist my clients in implementing interventions that may bring them a state of health.

My focus is on nutrition, gut health, and supplement use for mental health and wellness.

Today I strive to offer hope to my clients that there are solutions to our mental health struggles. My goal is to work collaboratively with my clients in identifying these solutions and to assist my clients in implementing interventions that may bring them a state of health.

content.raw: I am a board-certified Psychiatrist practicing Integrative Psychiatry. With over 20 years of clinical experience, I have gained expertise in treating a wide range of mental health conditions.  I am committed to providing compassionate, patient-centered care using a holistic approach. Having trained with experts in the field of trauma, I specialize in trauma-related disorders and draw from a diverse toolkit of therapeutic modalities, including relational psychotherapy, parts-work, mindfulness based stress reduction, compassionate inquiry and ketamine assisted psychotherapy.  I am a certified ketamine medical provider with extensive training in psychedelic assisted psychotherapy. I am dedicated to providing a highly individualized approach to care, working with each patient to address their unique needs and achieve emotional well-being and personal growth through a combination of evidence-based treatments and complementary modalities.

content.raw:
I’m an integrative psychiatrist and psychotherapist who believes in the innate healing power of the mind, body, and spirit. I help adults who struggle with anxiety disorders, mood disorders, ADHD, and PTSD/trauma harness the power of their experiences and emotions to restore self-confidence, reconnect with their purpose, pursue fulfilling relationships, and accomplish their personal and professional goals with joy. I also have a special interest in working with healthcare professionals to heal from work related trauma and find fulfillment in their career again.

I believe that you are whole. Deep within you exists all you need to heal and grow. It’s my role, my mission, to help guide you back to that space. I combine traditional psychiatric treatments, such as medication and talk therapy with complimentary approaches such as mindfulness/meditation, supplements, lifestyle coaching, and psychedelic integration.

When working with me your individuality will be valued, your experience respected, and your authenticity encouraged. If you are in search of a trusted guide to your mental health who is open minded, nonjudgmental, listens to you, and communicates directly than you are in the right place. I'm here to help. Visit my website to book a free 15 minute call.

title.rendered: Rhonda Parker, DNP, APRN, FNP-C, PMHNP-BC, PAT

content.raw:
I am a certified FNP, PMHNP and Psychedelic Assisted Therapy Provider. I own Mountain Healing, a retreat style practice designed to help Healthcare and Mental Health providers process compassion fatigue and burnout. Participants have the opportunity to learn the basics of psychedelic assisted therapy, in addition to topics on Mind Body Connection and Mindfulness. 
Mountain Healing is also a free standing clinic open to everyone to explore Ketamine and Esketamine as well as integrative approaches to optimize mental wellness.

content.raw:
I view therapy as a collaboration where therapist and client work together to connect with an inner healing intelligence and unconditional positive regard within each of us.

We can then tend to trauma, grief, resentments, and limiting beliefs that are contributing to symptoms.

With humility and reverence for the resilience, courage and vulnerability it can take to heal ourselves, I create a nonjudgemental, kind and compassionate space with room for humor, absurdity and fun.

I integrate healing modalities from various wisdom traditions that awaken our physiological and emotional connective systems in collaboration with the natural world.

This process can help clear historic trauma and family of origin wounding that is reinforced by a mass cultural amnesia.

I draw from evidence-based client-centered modalities including Transpersonal Psychology, Ecopsychology, Internal Family Systems, Gestalt, Family Systems and Somatic approaches to healing.

content.raw:
We have so much shit that we keep inside.
The longer that shit stays inside, the more it festers and grows.

It can lead to all sorts of problems and hardships-mental health, physical health, emotional health, energy imbalances, you name it.

The choice is yours: do you want to continue keeping that shit inside or do you want to finally find your inner peace and let that shit go?

The services I offer are based on my analysis of the cycle of growth and healing. 

I use a variety of clinical behavior analysis techniques including but not limited to ACT(Acceptance and Commitment Therapy), FAP (Functional Analytic Psychotherapy), and IBCT (Integrative Behavioral Couples Therapy), along with using the principles of ABA (Applied Behavior Analysis). 

I address  trauma/PTSD; intergenerational trauma; depression; anxiety; life transitions; grief; low self-esteem/self-love/self-trust; survivors of interpersonal violence/“gaslighting”; thriving in recovery; and people looking to level up in their lives.

content.raw: I was born in Odessa, Ukraine in 1975, while it was still part of the Soviet Union. In 1978, I immigrated to the United States with my parents and older brother. My parents were children of Holocaust survivors. Trauma and depression were very much prevalent in my family and community. However, the stigma of mental illness prevented any of my family members from seeking help. I grew up seeing the effects of trauma that resulted in dissolution of marriages and various addictions. I was always interested in these stories and initially thought my role was to tell them. I later realized my interests lie more in helping people reshape their stories and empower them to transform their lives.

content.raw: I'm an integrative psychiatric nurse practitioner with a background in clinical psychology. I have special interests in women's mental health, pregnancy, postpartum mood and anxiety issues, parenting, OCD, trauma, and 1st/2nd generation immigrant families. My goal is to collaborate with you as we investigate the root causes of your symptoms. My style is relational and connection-focused. I view you as a whole person, not a list of symptoms or diagnoses. Your treatment plan may include medications, supplements, nutrition and movement recommendations, social connection, stress management, sleep optimization, and mind-body skills, in order to optimize your mental health. In my work, I use my skills as a psychiatric nurse practitioner, my background in clinical psychology, and my continuing education in integrative psychiatry and reproductive psychiatry to support you on your journey to healing. Telehealth visits available in Oregon and Texas.

content.raw: A therapist in a private practice in Philadelphia, Pa., I have a masters in Counseling Psychology from Temple University and am licensed as a professional counselor in the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, with previous careers in newspaper journalism and sleep medicine. My background as a therapist includes community mental health and substance abuse treatment, and my interests include depression, anxiety, anger, life transitions, drug and alcohol dependence, creativity and relationships. In addition to psychedelics, my approach draws from Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Existential Therapy and parts work.
content.rendered: A therapist in a private practice in Philadelphia, Pa., I have a masters in Counseling Psychology from Temple University and am licensed as a professional counselor in the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, with previous careers in newspaper journalism and sleep medicine. My background as a therapist includes community mental health and substance abuse treatment, and my interests include depression, anxiety, anger, life transitions, drug and alcohol dependence, creativity and relationships. In addition to psychedelics, my approach draws from Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Existential Therapy and parts work.

content.raw: I’m a psychologist in private practice in Miami, Fl.  My clinical experience for 30 years has focused on treating trauma, loss, relationship issues, depression, anxiety, adult ADHD, and personal growth.   During the past twelve years I have gained training and experience in the use of mind altering/mind manifesting modalities which are now a primary focus of my work.

content.raw:
An advocate for compassionate, innovative health care, with nearly 47 years of clinical social work experience with 40 years working in various aspects of healthcare, Karen’s work as a clinician, educator, researcher, and psychotherapist focuses on palliative and end-of-life care in a wide range of health care settings serving culturally diverse populations. With warmth and deep, abiding respect layering her patient and family-centered care approach, Karen is committed to the relief of suffering, improving care and quality of life for individuals and their loved ones facing serious and advanced illness at all stages of life.
Karen has a keen interest in the intersection between contemplative medicine, neuroscience, trauma, and somatic therapies and most recently how psychedelics can support patients and providers in working with existential distress, and post traumatic growth - how these meet, address the health care disparities of racism and cultural differences as these areas have begun to inform each other and begin to address and improve treatment outcomes for all who need care.
Of her professional accomplishments, she is a Board Certified Advanced Palliative Care Social Worker. She co-developed the first intraprofessional collaborative provider team model known as the UCSF Symptom Management & Palliative Care Outpatient Services.
She initially retired from a 25 year career at UCSF in 2011 only to then consult and work with local Bay Area medical centers, i.e., PAMF, Marin General and for the past seven years, with Kaiser Permanente Medical Center, San Rafael, to co-develop and work with these institutions and providers to provide quality outpatient palliative care. She has recently been invited to return to UCSF to
continue her work through the Social Work Department with a focus on supporting the Department of Palliative Medicine and working as an advanced specialist and mentor clinician. She enjoys creative innovation in service provision and is now a part of a working group forming to explore development of a clinic linkage between psychiatry and geriatric medicine with a facet of treatment involving a Ketamine treatment clinic for elders
She continues to maintain a part-time private psychotherapy practice in SF, CA since 1986 to the present seeing adults and adolescents for individual, couples, and family therapy through a relational /attachment/systems approach, that is trauma-informed, socially engaged, with a somatic focus for various life challenging issues, inclusive of palliative care, serious illness, complex chronic medical conditions, health care disparities, moral injury and burnout in healthcare providers,.An increasing client population is interested in augmenting their personal therapy with plant medicines. Certified in psychedelic-assisted psychotherapy since 2022 from the Integrative Psychiatry Institute in Boulder, Colorado,, Karen works with several Physicians with Ketamine Treatment programs providing guiding and integration facets of treatment including trauma-informed stabilization if needed...  She has published in the fields of Sexual Therapy and Cancer Patients, Medical trauma and treatment in both pediatrics as well as adults, End of Life Care, Development of Outpatient Palliative Care clinic Models, Palliative Care and hospice provider distress, moral injury and moral resilience. 
At present , most of her private practice patient population are health care physicians and nurse providers at all stages in their careers who are coping with pervasive and persistent physical, cognitive, and spiritual distress due to working on covid frontline, addressing "edge states" of moral injury , complicated grief and suffering and those re-finding moral resilience in the midst

content.raw:
A Top Psychiatrist in Washington, a clinical professor at UW, and a best-selling author, Dr. Rozina Lakhani, brings hope to many people. 

Dr. Rozina provides patient-centered treatments for depression, anxiety, PTSD, and other psychiatric problems. These advanced treatments include TMS- Transcranial magnetic Stimulation, Spravato, medications and other integrative approaches. 

She and her colleagues at Shifa Health have been dedicated to reducing suffering from mental illness since 2003. 

Focusing on the whole person, she emphasizes empowering her patients/clients with knowledge. Her various mind training programs include:
* "Happy and Healthy Mind with Dr. Rozina," a weekly podcast
* Stress to Joy-books and courses for stress management
* Learning center with FAQ for people diagnosed with various mental disorders.

Her goal is to provide mental training tools for self-management besides treatment so you can live a happy and healthy life. For more information, visit https://shifahealth.org/rozina-lakhani/

content.raw:
Creative Arts Therapist, LCAT and Music Therapist, MT-BC based in Brooklyn, NY (traditional Lenape and Canarsie territories). Experience working with the Queer and TGNC community, as well as Poly/Kink identities and altered states.

Guided by healing justice and revolutionary growth principles, Laura’s approach to therapy incorporates trauma-informed and person-centered care. Using her trained musical ear, she guides clients to become aware of patterns and narratives through listening with curiosity and openness. Sessions with Laura are eclectic, combining verbal processing with somatic experiencing and creative exploration. She considers the therapeutic process co-work, emboldening clients to connect with their deepest selves while confronting both unique and broad oppressive structures. Above all, Laura is passionate about growth, agency, and resilience.

content.raw: Dr. Joshua Nead was drawn to Arizona's AT Still University School of Osteopathic Medicine for its approach to whole body health, which focuses on the connection between body, mind, and spirit. Dr. Nead utilizes a wide variety of non-medication, medication and psychotherapeutic approaches. Some of his specific interests include: ADHD, autism, attachment, depression, foster care, anxiety and trauma-informed care. His desire is to create a space where people can feel supported in their own personal journey to optimal mental health.

His desire is to create a space where people can feel supported in their own personal journey to optimal mental health.

content.raw: Julie Bremer is a board-certified Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner who has been nursing since 2005 in acute care, labor and delivery, newborn, and case management. She graduated from the University of Colorado, Anschutz, with a Master’s in Nursing degree focusing on psychiatric care throughout the lifespan. Julie believes in treating the whole person in a welcoming, caring, and non-judgmental presence. Her main area of focus includes mood and anxiety issues throughout the lifespan, including the perinatal period, using an integrative holistic approach. She uses medication management and motivational interviewing to devise a uniquely crafted plan of care where clients feel heard and empowered to take an active role in their well-being. Julie’s passion for working with the perinatal population led her to certification in Perinatal Mental Health through Postpartum Support International, where she volunteered as a peer support for several years. In her spare time, Julie enjoys creating art, being with her family, getting outside in nature, hiking, skiing, and rafting.

content.raw:
I believe that each of us deserves to live life as our best self. Often, shame and judgement about our problems can get in the way of solving them. Developing compassionate curiosity is key creating change in our lives. I believe that therapy can be a bridge to increased satisfaction and a sense of well-being. Undertaking therapeutic work in a safe, caring environment can allow you to understand yourself and your feelings, manage your stress, and change your strategies for coping. I am privileged to be involved in such important and transformative work.
I take a client-centered, trauma informed, integrative approach, believing that the therapeutic alliance is key to positive change. I draw on multiple theories (cognitive-behavioral, psychodynamic, ecological, feminist, humanistic) to understand my clients in the context of their lives. This allows me to tailor interventions to my clients' unique set of circumstances.
I am a native San Diegan, a veteran, and proud to give back to my community.  I have specialized education and training in gender & human sexualities, psychedelic assisted psychotherapy, and integrative behavioral health.  
I am currently providing preparation and integration services via telehealth for clients in California and Oregon.

content.raw:
As the founder of Storyline Counseling & Wellness, and a clinically Licensed Mental Health Counselor in the State of Washington, I feel deeply honored to be invited into peoples unique stories, helping them to listen to and sort through the heartbreak, shame, disappointment, and failure that they might be feeling.  My passion is to empower clients to live more integrated lives, by helping them to navigate their stories of harm with kindness and self-compassion instead of shame. I consider it a gift to have the opportunity to walk alongside you in whatever storyline you find yourself, in order to help you feel less stuck, and move towards greater freedom, wholeness, and hope. 

I received my training at The Seattle School of Theology and Psychology, where I earned a Masters of Arts in Counseling Psychology.  I am also certified in Life Span Integration.

My experience includes working with couples, groups and individuals of any age. I typically work with parents, teachers, leaders, artists, and young adults. My specific interests and specialties include trauma, family of origin issues, identity development, anxiety, depression, shame and relationship issues. I also value walking alongside clients who are in transitional seasons of life, as I believe these are often the seasons when our identity is challenged the most.

In my personal time, I enjoy spending time with my family, walking my dog, baking a nice artisan loaf of bread, drinking a hot cup of tea, and reading a good story.
I look forward to hearing from you.

I look forward to hearing from you.

content.raw: I have a private office in the Hartford CT area and have been in practice 30 years. I treat clients with depression, anxiety, addictions, and PTSD, using a variety of modalities. I have completed a certificate in psychedelic -assisted psychotherapy, including ketamine, MDMA, and psilocybin.  My goal is to partner with medical providers to assist clients with the integration work so essential to reaping the full rewards these medicines have to offer. I am dedicated to continuing my education ongoing to attain skills in multiple aspects of integration work, including internal family systems and various somatic approaches.

content.raw:
My name is Dr. Briana Komar. ​I am a board-certified psychiatrist with over a decade of clinical experience in both inpatient and outpatient settings. I am currently dedicating my career towards expanding and improving the field of precision medicine and believe that it is my duty as a psychiatrist to never stop learning, and to ceaselessly pursue knowledge of new treatment modalities through continuing education. I enjoy the challenge of helping people who do not respond to the “one-size-fits-all” approach of conventional medicine to find solutions to their problems.  I offer comprehensive bloodwork, as well as nutrigenomic, pharmacogenomic, and comprehensive hormonal testing in order to fully assess and optimize the health of my patients. Treatment may involve psychotherapy, lifestyle changes, use of medications, compounded hormones, nutraceuticals and supplements, and nutrition counseling.

I was raised in Brooklyn, New York and received my BA in Comparative Literature from Georgetown University.  After college, and prior to receiving my medical degree, I lived abroad in France and Ireland for several years, where I pursued an interest in the culinary arts and nutrition—working as a food writer and cheesemonger—an interest which I passionately pursue to this day. ​

Around that time I became intensely interested in women’s health issues, and returned to the United States to complete my premed requirements at Columbia University in New York. I earned my MD from St. George’s University School of Medicine, and then completed a residency in psychiatry at the University of Connecticut School of Medicine, serving my last year of residency as Chief Education Resident. I completed a fellowship in integrative psychiatry in 2021 at the Integrative Psychiatry Institute, an educational organization offering the most comprehensive evidence-based training in integrative psychiatry in the nation. I founded the Integrative Psychiatry Workshop in 2021 and became a certified personalized medicine practitioner in 2024.

content.raw:
This is your life. What are you going to make of it?  Oftentimes there are things holding us back from having the life we envision, from being the person we want to be. Amanda is committed to supporting your healing, growth and discovery so that you can uncover, reclaim and create the life you want and deserve. 

Amanda is based just outside of Denver, CO and has a private practice, Open Circle Counseling. She provides in-person ketamine assisted psychotherapy, as well as preparation and integration services, either in-person or online. Amanda also works with California residents via video and provides supervision to pre-licensed therapists.

She often assists adults working through challenges associated with anxiety, depression, trauma, high sensitivity and climate anxiety and grief--as well as spiritual and existential challenges.

types_of_therapy.raw: Expressive Arts,Humanistic,Integrative,Somatic,Strength-Based,Transpersonal

qualifications:
Amanda has over 20 years experience supporting clients and students in their healing and recovery. She began formally teaching yoga and meditation in 2000, studying with Ana Forrest. In 2005, she entered monastic life, studying vipassana meditation at Kyaswa Monastery in Burma. In 2010, she obtained her Master's in Counseling Psychology, with an emphasis in Expressive Arts Therapy, from California Institute of Integral Studies (CIIS). 

During her master's training, Amanda gained experience in gerontological wellness, and was trained on therapeutically working with non-ordinary states of consciousness within the elder population. Topics and techniques included: non-verbal processing, arts-based interventions, within a transpersonal, existential/humanistic frame. 

Amanda's own personal growth and healing via psychedelics lead her to become trained and certified as a psychedelic assisted psychotherapist. She draws from Buddhist teachings, yoga techniques, art and expression, as well as cognitive and behavioral approaches when working with clients. She is active in the psychedelic professional community and stays up-to-date on the evolving modality of psychedelic assisted therapy.

She is excited and passionate about assisting clients towards growth and healing. Reach out for a consultation to learn more via info@amandarebel.com or by calling 720-381-4110.

content.raw:
Thirty years a therapist, fourty years a client:  I am passionate about my work and my pending participation as a psilocybin-assisted therapy provider in my home state of Oregon.  While that process is pending further state-specific training, I happily provide preparation and integration sessions for individuals seeking healing and growth with psychedellics.  

Blending a vareity of wholistic and traditional approaches, I proudly work with the Deaf, hard of hearing, and LGBTQAI+ communities with a private practice in Portland, Oregon where in-person and virtual sessions are available  

I am also an independent licensed clinical social worker in Washington State and virtual sessions are available to those living in The Evergreen State.

content.raw:
I am a Los Angeles-based psychiatrist focused on holistic and integrative psychiatry and psychedelic integration. I work with people who are ready to take a new approach to their mental health and connect to their innate capacity for healing. Together, we address the root causes of symptoms, exploring everything from nutrition and inflammation to trauma and alienation.

I have trained with the leading physicians in evidence-based integrative and functional psychiatry, and I have studied with traditional medicine practitioners in China and Peru. My practice has been shaped by my work over the past decade with the psychedelic medicine ayahuasca, specifically with indigenous Shipibo healers in the Amazon.

I have trained with the leading physicians in evidence-based integrative and functional psychiatry, and I have studied with traditional medicine practitioners in China and Peru. My practice has been shaped by my work over the past decade with the psychedelic medicine ayahuasca, specifically with indigenous Shipibo healers in the Amazon.

I help patients from all backgrounds with their substance use disorders.
I am a board certified addiction doctor and medical acupuncturist.  I hold an IPI psychedelic psychotherapy certification. I expect to acquire MAPS certification, as well as my Oregon License for administering psilocybin as soon as they becomes available. My MPH is in forensic toxicology.

content.raw:
I am the founding therapists of Starks Therapy Group located in Oak Park, IL. I am a level 3 trained IFS therapist and have been working with clients in this modality for over 10 years. I graduated from the IPI PAP program in December of 2022.

Currently I am doing IV and IM Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy in partnership with AyaFusion Wellness and sublingual KAP in our office in Oak Park. We are also developing a KAP focused internship in 2023 as a practice. I provide prep and integration sessions with a harm reduction lens for folks doing their own medicine work. I provide IFS supervision and consultation on starting a thriving private or group practice. I work with client from a Health at Every Size perspective and am LGBTQIA+ affirming.

I, Autumn, do not accept insurance and am taking only adult clients at this time. The rest of the practice is in network with BCBS PPO IL and sees kids and teens as well. Clinicians on the team use approaches such as Art Therapy, EMDR, ACT, CBT, DBT, IFS and other modalities.
content.rendered:
I am the founding therapists of Starks Therapy Group located in Oak Park, IL. I am a level 3 trained IFS therapist and have been working with clients in this modality for over 10 years. I graduated from the IPI PAP program in December of 2022.

Currently I am doing IV and IM Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy in partnership with AyaFusion Wellness and sublingual KAP in our office in Oak Park. We are also developing a KAP focused internship in 2023 as a practice. I provide prep and integration sessions with a harm reduction lens for folks doing their own medicine work. I provide IFS supervision and consultation on starting a thriving private or group practice. I work with client from a Health at Every Size perspective and am LGBTQIA+ affirming.

I, Autumn, do not accept insurance and am taking only adult clients at this time. The rest of the practice is in network with BCBS PPO IL and sees kids and teens as well. Clinicians on the team use approaches such as Art Therapy, EMDR, ACT, CBT, DBT, IFS and other modalities.

types_of_therapy.raw: Acceptance and Commitment (ACT),Art Therapy,Clinical Supervision and Licensed Supervisors,Cognitive Behavioral (CBT),EMDR,Existential,Expressive Arts,Marital,Internal Family Systems (IFS),Interpersonal,Person-Centered,Psychedelic Therapy,Trauma Focused

qualifications:
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) and received my Masters of Social Work from Boston University. I received my Bachelor of the Arts in Psychology from Hope College in Holland, Michigan.

In addition to my academic studies, I have pursued training in Internal Family System (IFS Level 3 Graduate), Trauma-Focused Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (TF-CBT) and other creative/expressive therapies. I have a special clinical interest in Complex PTSD and other disorders resulting from religious trauma and spiritual abuse. I am certified in Psychedelic Assisted Psychotherapy through the Integrative Psychiatry Institute.

content.raw:
Your decision to evolve the way you see the world, and your place in it, is one of courage, vulnerability, and commitment. We sit together in presence and curiosity so that each session is a safe, nurturing space which allows you to hear and trust your inner healer and your own deep wisdom. We use our sessions to resolve chronic stressors, habits, and self-sabotaging life patterns so that you actively create and thrive in your authentic life.

Our work integrates Western and Eastern techniques including Jungian active imagination and journeying; soul retrieval; shadow work; Dialectical Behavior Therapy; psychedelic assisted therapy and integration; meditation; mantra; and somatic therapies. 

I offer groups for psychedelic medicine integration, DBT trauma skills, and psycho-spiritual awakening.

I address: trauma/C-PTSD; intergenerational trauma; depression; anxiety; life transitions; grief; religious trauma; psycho-spiritual awakening; low self-esteem/self-love/self-trust; toxic masculinity/femininity; survivors of interpersonal violence/“gaslighting”; thriving in recovery; and people looking to level up in their lives.

content.raw: Laurel received her nursing education from The Ohio State University and is board certified as a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner. She sees patients aged 12 and up in Ohio. Laurel focuses on trauma-responsive, person-centered diagnosis. and treatment and is advanced trained in Family Systems Trauma Therapy. She offers integrative evaluation and assessment, nutritional and botanical support alongside medication management for optimal health in her telehealth practice. Laurel is a Blomberg Rhythmic Movement Training (BRMT) consultant; practice of the BRMT protocol helps the brain make new and healthy connections through primitive reflex integration to overcome developmental deficits and difficulties brought on by infection, toxins, injury, and stress. Laurel also supports mental health in families, organizations, and corporations through facilitation of group drumming.
content.rendered: Laurel received her nursing education from The Ohio State University and is board certified as a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner. She sees patients aged 12 and up in Ohio. Laurel focuses on trauma-responsive, person-centered diagnosis. and treatment and is advanced trained in Family Systems Trauma Therapy. She offers integrative evaluation and assessment, nutritional and botanical support alongside medication management for optimal health in her telehealth practice. Laurel is a Blomberg Rhythmic Movement Training (BRMT) consultant; practice of the BRMT protocol helps the brain make new and healthy connections through primitive reflex integration to overcome developmental deficits and difficulties brought on by infection, toxins, injury, and stress. Laurel also supports mental health in families, organizations, and corporations through facilitation of group drumming.

job_title: Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner-Board Certified

title.raw: Pamela Krueger, PhD(c), MA, LMFTA, MFA, JD
title.rendered: Pamela Krueger, PhD(c), MA, LMFTA, MFA, JD

content.raw:
Pamela Krueger is a licensed marriage and family therapy associate and expressive arts psychotherapist. She completed her MA in Counseling Psychology with a concentration in expressive arts therapy. She also holds a master of fine arts in creative writing and a juris doctor degree and has extensive experience facilitating groups. She is currently enrolled in a doctoral program in expressive arts therapy.
She is Cancer Lifeline’s Clinical Program Administrator and also oversees its cancer support group program. Her therapeutic approach is grounded in an existential-humanistic-transpersonal lens with multicultural humility and a belief of the power of creative processes for healing. Trained in a variety of creative art modalities, she can offer clients a range of ways to connect with their inner knowing in ways that can support healing and growth.
content.rendered:

She is Cancer Lifeline’s Clinical Program Administrator and also oversees its cancer support group program. Her therapeutic approach is grounded in an existential-humanistic-transpersonal lens with multicultural humility and a belief of the power of creative processes for healing. Trained in a variety of creative art modalities, she can offer clients a range of ways to connect with their inner knowing in ways that can support healing and growth.

job_title: Clinical Program Administrator & Expressive Arts Psychotherapist

itemListElement.name: Pamela Krueger, PhD(c), MA, LMFTA, MFA, JD

content.raw:
I have had the honor of working with individuals in the Mental Health field for over 20 years. When I first became a  Psychiatric Nurse,  I was active duty in the United States Navy. Then I went into Community Mental Health and became a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner in 2012. I have specialized in addiction, child and adolescent psychiatry, and completed the Ketamine Provider training and Integrative Provider fellowship through IPI. 
What I particularly enjoy about the work I do is connecting with individuals around healing and feeling well.
content.rendered:
I have had the honor of working with individuals in the Mental Health field for over 20 years. When I first became a Psychiatric Nurse, I was active duty in the United States Navy. Then I went into Community Mental Health and became a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner in 2012. I have specialized in addiction, child and adolescent psychiatry, and completed the Ketamine Provider training and Integrative Provider fellowship through IPI.
What I particularly enjoy about the work I do is connecting with individuals around healing and feeling well.

In addition to her work as a therapist, Stacey is a volunteer researcher working with both the UCLA Semel Institute for Neuroscience and the Trance Science Research Institute in Paris, France. She is bilingual in English and French.

content.raw: Grounded by traditional studies in clinical psychology and contemplative neuroscience, I offer therapeutic guidance to individuals, small groups and organizations that integrates the guiding principles of yogic sciences with classical clinical psychology. . As a Certified-IAYT international yoga therapist my work is safe, grounded in contemporary methodologies and highly tailored to each clients unique needs. A life long learner, I strive to remain current in the latest research and developments in mental health care. I strive to provide the most informed guidance and education on the most effective options to consider as they navigate the array of mental heath care options and treatments available.

content.raw:
I take a comprehensive approach to treating mental health conditions and tailor treatment to meet the needs of my patients. My training is unique in that I have a masters degree as a psychotherapist in addition to being a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner. This,  along with additional training in integrative psychiatry, allows me to treat my patients holistically, addressing symptoms as well as root causes for mental health conditions.  I work with my patients to develop treatment plans that may include lifestyle changes, counseling, medication, and non pharmacological supplementation.
In person and telehealth visits available.

In person and telehealth visits available.

yoast_head_json.title: Leslie Harvey - IPI Directory
yoast_head_json.og_description: Co-Founder of group practice providing integrative psychedelic therapy in Texas and New Mexico.

content.raw:
I specialize in treating trauma and its consequences, including PTSD, depression, anxiety, substance abuse, chronic pain, and relationship stress. I'm trained in a variety of evidenced-based practices, including Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Dialectical Behavior Therapy.

With regards to my psychedelic work, I collaborate with clients to figure out what approach is best for them, while also being flexible as the medicine moves people to different psychological spaces. As a cisgender, gay, mixed race man, I recognize the importance of psychological safety and power differential when using psychedelics. Whether am I in the room during a medicine session, or talking with a client afterwards for integration, trust and safety are tantamount to making progress with psychedelics. I welcome queer individuals from all walks of life and relationship statuses in my work.
content.rendered:

With regards to my psychedelic work, I collaborate with clients to figure out what approach is best for them, while also being flexible as the medicine moves people to different psychological spaces. As a cisgender, gay, mixed race man, I recognize the importance of psychological safety and power differential when using psychedelics. Whether am I in the room during a medicine session, or talking with a client afterwards for integration, trust and safety are tantamount to making progress with psychedelics. I welcome queer individuals from all walks of life and relationship statuses in my work.

content.raw: Rick is a Board Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist (CRNA) and takes a holistic integrative approach to health, wellness, life and patient care at AyaFusion Wellness Clinic. He obtained his bachelor’s degree from the University of Nebraska Medical Center and Masters of Biology specializing in anesthesia from the University of Missouri. He is also a member of the American Society of Ketamine Physicians, Psychotherapists, & Practitioners (ASKP3) and an active Certified Psychedelic Assisted Therapy Provider of the Integrative Psychiatry Institute.

content.raw:
Saré Gebhardt is a psychotherapist currently working in Lewiston, Maine. Musically trained at Berklee College of Music and completed her master's at Lesley University for Expressive Arts Therapy & Clinical Mental Health Counseling, Saré seeks to bring awareness about the benefits of the arts into the therapeutic space. She maintains a state license of a Clinical Professional Counselor. She has trained in a variety of settings, from psychiatric hospitals, school systems, Center for Grieving Children, homes with families in crisis and Residential Treatment. Saré has also participated in multi-disciplinary teams in statewide agencies. She maintains membership with Association for Play Therapy and International Expressive Arts Therapy Association.

Saré is trained for work with individuals (children, adolescents and adults), groups, and families. Saré has experience in the areas of grief, trauma, depression, stress management, anxiety, relationships,  sexuality and gender identity and various issues with individuals with disabilities. Saré has extensive training in the use of expressive arts therapy, including the following modalities: music, art, drama, writing, poetry, sandtray, play and movement. Along with this specialized counseling, Saré has also completed trainings at the Gestalt International Study Center. Saré has completed her EMDRIA-approved EMDR training with specialty training in children along with TBRI training (Trust-Based Relational Intervention).

content.raw:
I am a Licensed Mental Health Counselor in the state of Washington and a Licensed Clinical Professional Counselor in the state of Idaho and I am working toward licensure in Oregon. I graduated with a master of arts in counseling from the Seattle School of Theology and Psychology. My undergraduate degree is a B.S. in Special Education from Vanderbilt University. I taught students with multiple and severe disabilities for 3 years before pursuing my counseling degree.

I have journeyed with clients who have a wide variety of concerns including depression, anxiety, trauma, and sexual abuse. I specialize in the treatment of trauma, using EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) as well as Ketamine Assisted Therapy. I am a Certified Psychedelic Assisted Therapy Provider through Integrative Psychiatry Institute. Inquire if you are interested in learning more.

I have maintained a private practice since 2002 beginning in Washington, and continuing in Idaho from 2012 to present. I primarily work with adults dealing with trauma, anxiety, and depression.

content.raw:
I am a Licensed Mental Health Counselor in the state of Washington and a Licensed Clinical Professional Counselor in the state of Idaho. I received my M.A. in Counseling from Seattle School of Theology and Psychology, and I have had a counseling practice with my wife, Ashlie since 2002.

I have journeyed with clients struggling with depression, anxiety, addictions, grief, chronic illness, divorce, self-harm, and suicidal thoughts. I specialize in the treatment of trauma, working mostly with adults.

I use EMDR therapy to help people work through their hardest experiences, so they can live more rewarding lives. EMDR stands for Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing and is a form of therapy that helps people heal from trauma or other distressing life experiences.

Experiencing trauma can block the brain’s natural way of recovering from distressing events. EMDR therapy is designed to reactivate this natural healing process through alternating eye movements, sounds, or taps. I have seen how amazing EMDR is for my clients! Watching their healing process with EMDR has made me more confident that I can help future clients with their pain.

Ketamine Assisted Therapy is another powerful intervention in the treatment of trauma and depression. I recently received training and certification in this field during an intensive year-long program through Integrative Psychiatry Institute. Inquire if you are interested in learning more.

EMDR therapy is designed to reactivate this natural healing process through alternating eye movements, sounds, or taps. I have seen how amazing EMDR is for my clients! Watching their healing process with EMDR has made me more confident that I can help future clients with their pain.

Ketamine Assisted Therapy is another powerful intervention in the treatment of trauma and depression. I recently received training and certification in this field during an intensive year-long program through Integrative Psychiatry Institute. Inquire if you are interested in learning more.
